Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First, I need to identify patterns or phases in the readings. Let me list out the events in order and note the key changes. Events 1-3: Temperature around 20-21C, pressure 1 bar, vibration 0, material flow 0, tool position 0, energy around 0.4-0.5 kWh. This seems like an idle or standby state no activity, low energy, no material flow. Events 4-6: Temperature jumps to 50-58C, pressure increases to 1.5 bar, vibration spikes to 30-38 Hz, material flow becomes 5 units/s, tool position moves to 5-10 mm, energy rises to 2.0-3.0 kWh. This looks like an active phase with high energy and vibration, likely a cutting or machining operation. The tool is moving and material is flowing. Event 7-8: Temperature drops to 40C, pressure up to 2 bar, vibration down to 10 Hz, material flow reduces to 2 units/s, tool position stays at 15 mm, energy at 2.0 kWh. Conditions are still active but less intense. Maybe this is a different phase, like assembling or joining? The tool is stationary at 15 mm, so perhaps its holding or pressing. Event 9: Temperature spikes to 80C, pressure remains 2 bar, vibration jumps to 50 Hz, material flow drops to 0, tool position still 15 mm, energy peaks at 5.0 kWh. This is a sudden high-energy, high-vibration event with no material flow. Could be welding high heat and vibration, but no material feed. Event 10: Temperature plummets to 30C, pressure back to 1 bar, vibration very low at 1 Hz, material flow 0, tool position 15 mm, energy drops to 1.0 kWh. After the high-energy event, things cool down quickly. Maybe this is a cooldown or inspection phase. Events 11-12: Temperature 25C, pressure 1 bar, vibration 5 Hz, material flow starts at 1 unit/s, tool position moves to 20 mm, energy 1.5-1.6 kWh. Material flow resumes, tool moves perhaps packaging or final assembly? The tool is moving to a new position, and material is flowing slowly. Events 13-15: Back to baseline: temperature 20-21C, pressure 1 bar, vibration 0, material flow 0, tool position 0, energy 0.4-0.5 kWh. Machine is idle again, ready for the next cycle.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now, grouping these into activities: 1. Idle/Standby: Events 1-3 and 13-15. Low readings across the board, no activity. 2. Cutting/ Machining: Events 4-6. High temperature, vibration, material flow, tool movement classic signs of cutting metal. 3. Assembling/Joining: Events 7-8. Moderate conditions, tool stationary at 15 mm, material flow reduced. Could be pressing or assembling parts. 4. Welding: Event 9. Extreme temperature and vibration spike, no material flow, tool stationary. Welding fits high heat and vibration. 5. Quality Inspection/Cooldown: Event 10. Rapid cooldown, low vibration, no material flow. After welding, inspection or cooling down. 6. Packaging/Finishing: Events 11-12. Material flow resumes, tool moves to 20 mm, moderate energy. Likely packaging or final touches.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I need to assign labels and rationales. Let me double-check if the transitions make sense. After cutting, tool moves to 15 mm for assembling, then welding at same position, then cooldown, then packaging with tool moving to 20 mm. That seems logical.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Also, note that pressure is mostly 1-2 bar, not varying much, so maybe its not a key indicator here. Vibration and temperature are the main drivers.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Finally, output the labeled activities with explanations.
